content_markdown:
A cell of emf $6.0\text{ V}$ and negligible internal resistance is connected to three resistors as shown. The resistors have resistance of $3.0\text{ }\Omega$ and $6.0\text{ }\Omega$ as shown.

What is the current in resistor X?

diagram_metadata.description: A circuit diagram showing a 6.0V cell connected in series with resistor X (3.0 ohms). Resistor X is then in series with a parallel combination of two 6.0 ohm resistors.

vibe_explanation: First, calculate the equivalent resistance of the parallel section: two $6.0\text{ }\Omega$ resistors in parallel give $R_p = \frac{6.0}{2} = 3.0\text{ }\Omega$. The total resistance of the circuit is the sum of resistor X and the parallel section: $R_{total} = 3.0\text{ }\Omega + 3.0\text{ }\Omega = 6.0\text{ }\Omega$. Using Ohm's law for the whole circuit, $I = \frac{V}{R_{total}} = \frac{6.0\text{ V}}{6.0\text{ }\Omega} = 1.0\text{ A}$. Since resistor X is in series with the rest of the circuit, the total current flows through it.
